papers.title_en: GPU-Accelerated Continuous-Time Successive Convexification for Contact-Implicit Legged Locomotion
papers.evidence: 足式机器人运动的接触隐式轨迹优化
papers.tldr: 本研究提出了ci-SCvx框架，旨在解决接触隐式轨迹优化中离散化精度与计算效率的矛盾。通过引入积分交叉互补约束，该方法在连续时间下处理多体动力学与碰撞，避免了离散节点间接触事件的遗漏。利用JAX进行GPU加速和自定义解析器优化，ci-SCvx在保持物理一致性的同时，求解速度比现有主流方法快一个数量级以上，显著提升了足式机器人运动规划的实时性。
papers.abstract_en: Contact-implicit trajectory optimization (CITO) enables the automatic discovery of contact sequences, but most methods rely on fine time discretization to capture all contact events accurately, which increases problem size and runtime while tying solution quality to grid resolution. We extend the recently proposed sequential convex programming (SCP) approach for trajectory optimization, continuous-time successive convexification (ct-SCvx), to CITO by introducing integral cross-complementarity constraints, which eliminate the risk of missing contact events between discretization nodes while preserving the flexibility of contact mode changes. The resulting framework, contact-implicit successive convexification (ci-SCvx), models full multibody dynamics in maximal coordinates, including stick-slip friction and partially elastic impacts. To handle complementarity constraints, we embed a backtracking homotopy scheme within SCP for reliable convergence. We implement this framework in a stand-alone Python software, leveraging JAX for GPU acceleration and a custom canonical-form parser for the convex subproblems of SCP to avoid the overhead of general-purpose modeling tools such as CVXPY. We demonstrate ci-SCvx on diverse legged-locomotion tasks. In particular, we validate the approach in MuJoCo with the Gymnasium HalfCheetah model against the MuJoCo MPC baseline, showing that a tracking simulation with the optimized torque profiles from ci-SCvx produces physically consistent trajectories with lesser energy consumption. We also show that the resulting software achieves faster solve times than existing state-of-the-art SCP implementations by over an order of magnitude, thereby demonstrating a practically important contribution to scalable real-time trajectory optimization.

papers.title_en: Sumo: Dynamic and Generalizable Whole-Body Loco-Manipulation
papers.authors: John Z. Zhang, Maks Sorokin, Jan Brüdigam, Brandon Hung, Stephen Phillips, Dmitry Yershov, Farzad Niroui, Tong Zhao, Leonor Fermoselle, Xinghao Zhu, Chao Cao, Duy Ta, Tao Pang, Jiuguang Wang, Preston Culbertson, Zachary Manchester, Simon Le Cléac'h
papers.evidence: Spot四足机器人的全身移动操纵
papers.tldr: 本研究提出Sumo框架，旨在解决腿式机器人对大型重物的动态全身操作难题。通过在测试时利用采样规划器对预训练的全身控制策略进行引导，实现了无需额外微调即可泛化至多种任务的能力。在Spot机器人上的实测证明，该方法能处理超过其额定载荷的轮胎和大型障碍物，并成功扩展至类人机器人的仿真任务。
papers.abstract_en: This paper presents a sim-to-real approach that enables legged robots to dynamically manipulate large and heavy objects with whole-body dexterity. Our key insight is that by performing test-time steering of a pre-trained whole-body control policy with a sample-based planner, we can enable these robots to solve a variety of dynamic loco-manipulation tasks. Interestingly, we find our method generalizes to a diverse set of objects and tasks with no additional tuning or training, and can be further enhanced by flexibly adjusting the cost function at test time. We demonstrate the capabilities of our approach through a variety of challenging loco-manipulation tasks on a Spot quadruped robot in the real world, including uprighting a tire heavier than the robot's nominal lifting capacity and dragging a crowd-control barrier larger and taller than the robot itself. Additionally, we show that the same approach can be generalized to humanoid loco-manipulation tasks, such as opening a door and pushing a table, in simulation. Project code and videos are available at https://sumo.rai-inst.com/.

papers.title_en: Natural Gradient Gaussian Approximation Filter on Lie Groups for Robot State Estimation
papers.authors: Tianyi Zhang, Wenhan Cao, Chang Liu, Yao Lyu, Shengbo Eben Li
papers.evidence: 基于李群的足式机器人状态估计
papers.tldr: 针对足式机器人等在李群流形上的状态估计问题，本文提出了一种名为 NANO-L 的新型滤波器。该方法将滤波问题重构为高斯增量变量的参数优化问题，通过自然梯度法和费舍尔信息矩阵处理流形曲率，从而避免了传统滤波器的局部线性化误差。实验证明，该算法在保持高效计算的同时，显著提升了复杂地形下的估计精度。
papers.abstract_en: Accurate state estimation for robotic systems evolving on Lie group manifolds, such as legged robots, is a prerequisite for achieving agile control. However, this task is challenged by nonlinear observation models defined on curved manifolds, where existing filters rely on local linearization in the tangent space to handle such nonlinearity, leading to accumulated estimation errors. To address this limitation, we reformulate manifold filtering as a parameter optimization problem over a Gaussian-distributed increment variable, thereby avoiding linearization. Under this formulation, the increment can be mapped to the Lie group through the exponential operator, where it acts multiplicatively on the prior estimate to yield the posterior state. We further propose a natural gradient optimization scheme for solving this problem, whose iteration process leverages the Fisher information matrix of the increment variable to account for the curvature of the tangent space. This results in an iterative algorithm named the Natural Gradient Gaussian Approximation on Lie Groups (NANO-L) filter. Leveraging the perturbation model in Lie derivative, we prove that for the invariant observation model widely adopted in robotic localization tasks, the covariance update in NANO-L admits an exact closed-form solution, eliminating the need for iterative updates thus improving computational efficiency. Hardware experiments on a Unitree GO2 legged robot operating across different terrains demonstrate that NANO-L achieves approximately 40% lower estimation error than commonly used filters at a comparable computational cost.

papers.title_en: Simulator Adaptation for Sim-to-Real Learning of Legged Locomotion via Proprioceptive Distribution Matching
papers.evidence: 通过本体感受分布匹配进行足式机器人运动的从仿真到现实学习
papers.tldr: 针对足式机器人从仿真到现实迁移中的动力学差异问题，本文提出了一种基于本体感受分布匹配的仿真器自适应方法。该方法通过比较硬件和仿真中关节观测与动作的分布，无需时间对齐或外部传感器即可优化仿真参数。在Go2机器人上的实验表明，该方法仅需不到5分钟的硬件数据即可显著减少运动漂移，在双足行走等挑战性任务中表现优异，为仿真器校准提供了一种实用且高效的方案。
papers.abstract_en: Simulation trained legged locomotion policies often exhibit performance loss on hardware due to dynamics discrepancies between the simulator and the real world, highlighting the need for approaches that adapt the simulator itself to better match hardware behavior. Prior work typically quantify these discrepancies through precise, time-aligned matching of joint and base trajectories. This process requires motion capture, privileged sensing, and carefully controlled initial conditions. We introduce a practical alternative based on proprioceptive distribution matching, which compares hardware and simulation rollouts as distributions of joint observations and actions, eliminating the need for time alignment or external sensing. Using this metric as a black-box objective, we explore adapting simulator dynamics through parameter identification, action-delta models, and residual actuator models. Our approach matches the parameter recovery and policy-performance gains of privileged state-matching baselines across extensive sim-to-sim ablations on the Go2 quadruped. Real-world experiments demonstrate substantial drift reduction using less than five minutes of hardware data, even for a challenging two-legged walking behavior. These results demonstrate that proprioceptive distribution matching provides a practical and effective route to simulator adaptation for sim-to-real transfer of learned legged locomotion.

papers.authors: Yiru Yang, Zhuojie Wu, Quentin Marguet, Nishant Kumar Singh, Max Schulthess
papers.evidence: 用于机器人感知和语义理解的4D重建
papers.tldr: 针对动态场景4D重建中几何感知与语义理解脱节的问题，本文提出DINO_4D。该方法通过引入冻结的DINOv3特征作为结构先验，将语义意识注入重建过程，有效抑制了动态跟踪中的语义漂移。在保持线性时间复杂度的同时，显著提升了跟踪精度和重建完整性，为构建兼具几何精度与语义理解的4D世界模型提供了新范式。
papers.abstract_en: In the intersection of computer vision and robotic perception, 4D reconstruction of dynamic scenes serve as the critical bridge connecting low-level geometric sensing with high-level semantic understanding. We present DINO\_4D, introducing frozen DINOv3 features as structural priors, injecting semantic awareness into the reconstruction process to effectively suppress semantic drift during dynamic tracking. Experiments on the Point Odyssey and TUM-Dynamics benchmarks demonstrate that our method maintains the linear time complexity $O(T)$ of its predecessors while significantly improving Tracking Accuracy (APD) and Reconstruction Completeness. DINO\_4D establishes a new paradigm for constructing 4D World Models that possess both geometric precision and semantic understanding.

papers.title_en: Ψ-Map: Panoptic Surface Integrated Mapping Enables Real2Sim Transfer
papers.authors: Xuan Yu, Yuxuan Xie, Changjian Jiang, Shichao Zhai, Rong Xiong, Yu Zhang, Yue Wang
papers.evidence: 用于机器人感知的全景表面集成制图
papers.tldr: 本研究提出Ψ-Map框架，旨在解决大规模场景下3D高斯泼溅在几何精度、全景理解和实时性方面的挑战。通过结合LiDAR约束的2D高斯面元表示、端到端查询引导的全景学习架构以及优化的渲染策略，该系统实现了高精度的表面重建和全局一致的语义理解。实验证明，该方法在保持超过40 FPS的实时推理速度的同时，显著提升了重建质量，为机器人Real2Sim迁移提供了强有力的感知支持。
papers.abstract_en: Open-vocabulary panoptic reconstruction is essential for advanced robotics perception and simulation. However, existing methods based on 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) often struggle to simultaneously achieve geometric accuracy, coherent panoptic understanding, and real-time inference frequency in large-scale scenes. In this paper, we propose a comprehensive framework that integrates geometric reinforcement, end-to-end panoptic learning, and efficient rendering. First, to ensure physical realism in large-scale environments, we leverage LiDAR data to construct plane-constrained multimodal Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) and employ 2D Gaussian surfels as the map representation, enabling high-precision surface alignment and continuous geometric supervision. Building upon this, to overcome the error accumulation and cumbersome cross-frame association inherent in traditional multi-stage panoptic segmentation pipelines, we design a query-guided end-to-end learning architecture. By utilizing a local cross-attention mechanism within the view frustum, the system lifts 2D mask features directly into 3D space, achieving globally consistent panoptic understanding. Finally, addressing the computational bottlenecks caused by high-dimensional semantic features, we introduce Precise Tile Intersection and a Top-K Hard Selection strategy to optimize the rendering pipeline. Experimental results demonstrate that our system achieves superior geometric and panoptic reconstruction quality in large-scale scenes while maintaining an inference rate exceeding 40 FPS, meeting the real-time requirements of robotic control loops.

papers.title_en: ABot-Claw: A Foundation for Persistent, Cooperative, and Self-Evolving Robotic Agents
papers.authors: Dongjie Huo, Haoyun Liu, Guoqing Liu, Dekang Qi, Zhiming Sun, Maoguo Gao, Jianxin He, Yandan Yang, Xinyuan Chang, Feng Xiong, Xing Wei, Zhiheng Ma, Mu Xu
papers.evidence: 用于长时多机器人执行的具身控制架构
papers.tldr: 针对具身智能在开放环境中高层推理与底层执行脱节、缺乏长时闭环控制等问题，本文提出 ABot-Claw 框架。作为 OpenClaw 的具身扩展，它集成了异构机器人协调接口、多模态记忆系统和基于评论者的闭环反馈机制。通过解耦架构，ABot-Claw 实现了从自然语言意图到物理动作的闭环，支持机器人在动态环境中的持久协作与自我进化。
papers.abstract_en: Current embodied intelligent systems still face a substantial gap between high-level reasoning and low-level physical execution in open-world environments. Although Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models provide strong perception and intuitive responses, their open-loop nature limits long-horizon performance. Agents incorporating System 2 cognitive mechanisms improve planning, but usually operate in closed sandboxes with predefined toolkits and limited real-system control. OpenClaw provides a localized runtime with full system privileges, but lacks the embodied control architecture required for long-duration, multi-robot execution. We therefore propose ABot-Claw, an embodied extension of OpenClaw that integrates: 1) a unified embodiment interface with capability-driven scheduling for heterogeneous robot coordination; 2) a visual-centric cross-embodiment multimodal memory for persistent context retention and grounded retrieval; and 3) a critic-based closed-loop feedback mechanism with a generalist reward model for online progress evaluation, local correction, and replanning. With a decoupled architecture spanning the OpenClaw layer, shared service layer, and robot embodiment layer, ABot-Claw enables real-world interaction, closes the loop from natural language intent to physical action, and supports progressively self-evolving robotic agents in open, dynamic environments.

papers.title_en: Optimal Kinodynamic Motion Planning Through Anytime Bidirectional Heuristic Search with Tight Termination Condition
papers.authors: Yi Wang, Bingxian Mu, Shahab Shokouhi, May-Win Thein
papers.evidence: 用于机器人基准测试的动力学约束运动规划算法
papers.tldr: 本研究提出BTIT*算法，这是一种渐近最优的动力学采样运动规划算法。它首次将随时双向启发式搜索（Bi-HS）与批处理采样相结合，并引入了紧凑的终止条件以实现提前终止。该算法保证了“中间相遇”属性和最优性，在处理高维动力学系统（如4D双积分器和10D无人机模型）时，比传统非延迟启发式批处理规划器具有更快的首次解生成速度和更好的收敛性能。
papers.abstract_en: This paper introduces Bidirectional Tight Informed Trees (BTIT*), an asymptotically optimal kinodynamic sampling-based motion planning algorithm that integrates an anytime bidirectional heuristic search (Bi-HS) and ensures the \emph{meet-in-the-middle} property (MMP) and optimality (MM-optimality). BTIT* is the first anytime MEET-style algorithm to utilize termination conditions that are efficient to evaluate and enable early termination \emph{on-the-fly} in batch-wise sampling-based motion planning. Experiments show that BTIT* achieves strongly faster time-to-first-solution and improved convergence than representative \emph{non-lazy} informed batch planners on two kinodynamic benchmarks: a 4D double-integrator model and a 10D linearized Quadrotor. The source code is available here.

papers.title_en: Distributed State Estimation for Discrete-Time Systems With Unknown Inputs: An Optimization Approach
papers.authors: Ruixuan Zhao, Guitao Yang, Nicola Bastianello, Boli Chen
papers.evidence: 具有未知输入的规模系统分布式状态估计
papers.tldr: 本文提出一种针对具有局部未知输入的离散时间大规模系统的分布式未知输入观测器（DUIO）框架。该框架通过让各节点先利用局部数据估计部分可重构状态，再利用分布式优化算法进行全局信息融合，实现了对系统状态的准确估计。研究不仅证明了估计误差的有界性，还通过引入归一化步骤解决了系统病态几何条件下的收敛问题，为分布式状态估计提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: This paper proposes a novel Distributed Unknown Input Observer (DUIO) framework for state estimation in large-scale systems subject to local unknown inputs. We consider systems where outputs are measured by a network of spatially distributed sensors and inputs are introduced through multiple dispersed channels. In this framework, each local node utilizes only its local input and output measurements to estimate the maximal locally reconstructible state. Subsequently, nodes collaboratively reconstruct the whole system state via a distributed optimization algorithm that fuses these partial estimates. We provide a rigorous analysis showing that the estimation error is bounded, with the error bound explicitly dependent on the number of communication iterations per time step and strongly convexity constant determined by the system parameters. Furthermore, to counteract curvature anisotropy induced by poor conditioned system geometry, we embed a normalization step into the distributed optimization procedure. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework and the performance improvements yielded by the normalization procedure.
